notes: init writes WorkflowOverride{} and only sets fields for passed flags (no defaults frozen in); built-in defaults live solely in DefaultWorkflow (engine/workflow_resolve.go:12-19) applied at read time via ResolveWorkflowLayers.

WriteTaskFile (model/io.go:76-91) marshals the presence-tracked WorkflowOverride so absence stays absent; TestReadWriteTaskFile_RoundTrip (15-103) and NoWorkflowKeyNormalizesToEmpty (105-118) assert presence is preserved and a missing key normalizes to {}.

Effective reads clamp a copy of the override for resolution (EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile) while storage is untouched; parseWorkflowOverride (engine/projectconfig.go:86-125) preserves every present field by value; no write path injects or strips defaults. init.go:68 no longer materializes.

TaskFile.Workflow is the pointer-field WorkflowOverride, distinct from resolved Workflow; effective reads route only through engine.EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile (cli/gate.go:49,69) and engine.ResolveWorkflow (cli/review_status.go:28). done.go/plan.go have zero workflow references now. No new command/flag added.
set command registers only --bulk/--workflow/--project/--local; no --unset flag exists, and runSetWorkflow only adds/updates fields.
ResolveWorkflowLayers keeps task-override > project > built-in precedence unchanged; ProjectConfig schema (model/projectconfig.go:10-12) still the single top-level workflow key.
set and config (cli/config.go:22-32) flag sets are unchanged; no new command or flag introduced by this release.

StripTaskWorkflowFields is invoked only by tp config --extract (cli/config_extract.go), a user-invoked step; ReadTaskFile/WriteTaskFile never thin, clampWorkflowRanges affects only in-memory resolution. No automatic thinning path exists.
DefaultWorkflow remains the sole in-code definition of built-in defaults; unchanged by this release.

init --commit-strategy flag (init.go:33) still writes commit_strategy; the field is retained as a presence-tracked *string in WorkflowOverride (model/projectconfig.go:39) and round-tripped. Neither flag nor field removed.

editableWorkflowFields = five integers + checks (unchanged); readOnlyWorkflowFields = quality_gate, commit_strategy, rejected by runSetWorkflow (set.go:292-296). Settable set and read-only status preserved.

CommitStrategy *string on WorkflowOverride (model/projectconfig.go:39); parseWorkflowOverride reads commit_strategy (engine/projectconfig.go:99-105); ResolveWorkflowLayers resolves via pickString (configresolve.go:176). Test covers parse + 3-tier resolution. Note (out of this task's scope): WorkflowOverride.IsEmpty() omits CommitStrategy (model/projectconfig.go:50-58, tests-only usage), and config.go resolvedConfig omits commit_strategy so §10.7's config --resolved source:override clause is unimplemented.

TaskFile.Workflow stored as WorkflowOverride; ReadTaskFile/WriteTaskFile (model/io.go:11-91) apply no defaults on unmarshal and marshal only set fields, workflow key always an object. Effective reads go through the resolver (gate.go:49,69; review_status.go:28). io_test.go:15-118 covers {}, populated (quality_gate+commit_strategy+checks:[]), and no-key normalization; no RMW materializes a default.

ResolveEffectiveWorkflow/EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile clamp the task override before layering (configresolve.go:142,156). Tests: no-project-config resolves to built-in 2 with an 'out of range' warning; project-config review_clean_rounds:3 resolves to 3 (clamp never masks the project layer). Resolve returns nil error so the command exits 0.

init writes WorkflowOverride{} and sets quality_gate/commit_strategy only when the flag is non-empty (init.go:68-83). Table test asserts all four cases: no flags -> {}, quality-gate only, commit-strategy only, both composed, each with exactly the flagged fields and no materialized siblings.

runSetWorkflow (set.go:272-383) validates all pairs before reading/writing the file, sets only the named pointer field (no siblings). Out-of-range/non-parseable -> ExitValidation(1); unknown/non-settable incl. quality_gate/commit_strategy -> ExitUsage(2). Tests confirm exit 1/2 and file left unmodified on rejection; settable set is 5 ints + checks.

resolveConfigWorkflow clamps the override (config.go:120) before resolvedConfig attributes source via override.<field>!=nil (config.go:50-63), so a present-but-out-of-range field reports source project/default, never override. Test: file review_clean_rounds:0 + project 3 -> value 3, source 'project' (no masking + clamped source). In-range override->'override' is the preserved v0.24 path.

spec/0.25.0.tasks.json workflow block is thinned to "workflow": {} (line 6) and committed (not in git working-tree status). The pre/post config --resolved equivalence check is a process step; the observable artifact is the value-preserving thinned block.

ClampWorkflowRanges(&override) at L120 only dereferences pointer fields under a nil guard (projectconfig.go L20). Its []string warnings are intentionally dropped because they were already surfaced at L115-117; re-emitting would double-print. override read at L103 is a guarded best-effort read (config is read-only, no task-file write path). No nil-deref, no lock-free write.

Test-only. All os.WriteFile calls are checked with require.NoError and use 0o600 perms (L21,L24) and filepath.Join (L18-24). Type assertions at L28-29 are test-fatal on failure, acceptable. No prod write/lock path.

engine.EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile(taskFilePath) at L136 and L341 returns a Workflow value; .QualityGate is a plain string, no nil-deref. The task file was already read successfully at L126 (single-ID) so the internal best-effort read is safe. Read-only resolution, no write/lock change.
Test-only. json.Marshal error checked with require.NoError (L21). No file writes, no locks, no swallowed errors.
Test-only. All os.WriteFile use 0o600 and filepath.Join (L15,L31,L42,L58,L71,L85); every read/write checked with require.NoError. Type assertions (L49,L51) are test-fatal, acceptable.

v0.26.0 change (L80 Workflow: model.WorkflowOverride{}) is safe. Workflow-preserve read at L126 guards on readErr==nil (documented best-effort). Path built with filepath.Join (L117-119); input validated. WriteTaskFile at L177 is lock-free but that is pre-existing create/overwrite semantics guarded by os.Stat (L140), not a v0.26.0 regression.

Pointer assigns take address of fresh locals qg/cs (L77-82), no aliasing (not in a loop). Path via filepath.Join (L52-54). runEjectRoles writes files 0o600 (L135), dirs 0o755, filepath.Join. WriteTaskFile at L85 is lock-free but pre-existing create semantics guarded by os.Stat existence check (L56); not a v0.26.0 regression. No swallowed errors.
Test-only. os.WriteFile/ReadFile/Unmarshal all checked with require.NoError; 0o600 perms (L30) and filepath.Join (L30,L35). No swallowed errors.

ResolveWorkflowLayers(tf.Workflow, ProjectWorkflowOverride(".")) at L160 handles nil override pointer fields via the pick* nil guards (configresolve.go L105-133). wfPtr = &wf takes the address of a local; read-only, no write/lock. No nil-deref.

strconv.Atoi error handled via convErr (L324-329); no swallowed errors. Pointer assigns use a fresh loop-local v:=val (L356) so &v has no loop-variable aliasing bug; &checksValue is a single-assigned var (L281,L372). Write occurs inside engine.WithFileLock (L346,L376). Input validated: field allow/deny lists (L292-301), int range (L330-335), ValidateChecks (L311).

resolveConfigWorkflow adds engine.ClampWorkflowRanges(&override) with a clear inline comment; the exported wrapper is documented at engine/projectconfig.go:33-35. Sound, no propagated error to %w-wrap, no slice.
New test has a descriptive doc comment (§3.4/§10.8), idiomatic require/assert, clear failure messages. No conventions issues.

Both sites (l.136 and l.341) correctly switch from tfPre.Workflow.QualityGate (now *string override) to engine.EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile(taskFilePath).QualityGate!="" (resolved string); also layers project config, which tfPre alone could not. Extra re-read is minor/acceptable. No dead code, no nil slice.
Tests updated to the resolved &model.Workflow{QualityGate:...} type (string field); descriptive names and assertions. Clean.
readPersistedWorkflow helper uses t.Helper(); well-structured subtests cover empty-block/force-preservation/imported-wins/empty-on-new (§3.6/§10.11). Idiomatic, no issues.
Workflow: model.WorkflowOverride{} replaces engine.DefaultWorkflow() — correct presence-preserving behavior (no materialized defaults on new bare-array import). No nil slice, no dead code.

Materialized-defaults Workflow{} block removed cleanly; sparse model.WorkflowOverride{} written; pointer assignment via local copy (qg:=initQualityGate; ...=&qg) is idiomatic. No dangling refs (build+vet clean).
New table-driven test with doc comment (§10.1) verifying no default materialization and flag composition. Clean, idiomatic.

wfPtr *model.Workflow resolved via ResolveWorkflowLayers only when !planMinimal; resolved Checks is non-nil (DefaultWorkflow seeds []Check{}; pickChecks returns it when layers absent), so plan output serializes checks as [] not null. Only theoretical nil path (hand-authored "checks":null via lenient project-config parser) is out of these files' scope and not producible by any tp command.

Presence-preserving pointer writes (v:=val; ...=&v), strconv.Atoi replaces fmt.Sscanf, error taxonomy switched to output.Error+os.Exit with correct codes; checksValue guarded non-nil (l.316-318); updated/results use make(). No nil slice reaches JSON. Minor pre-existing: runSetWorkflow ~113 lines, marginally over the ~110 handler tolerance, slightly extended by mechanical error-exit conversions — not a blocker.

tp config --resolved source labels correct (override/project/default via sourceLabel L37-46). §3.4 clamped-field attribution applied: resolveConfigWorkflow clamps override after resolution (L118-120) so an out-of-range field reports source project/default, never override. checks value never null (resolution seeds []Check{}); workflow key always emitted as a JSON object (struct, not pointer). Malformed config aborts exit 3.

Asserts §3.4/§10.8: out-of-range task override (review_clean_rounds:0) is clamped and reported as source=project value=3, never override. Covers the project branch; the default branch is covered by engine clamp_resolve_test.go.

Gate reads rerouted to engine.EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile (resolves + clamps) at L136/L341/L575 — QualityGate now comes from the resolution path, not the storage struct (§4.2). exit_code serialized null on timeout preserved; exit codes and JSON shape unchanged.

Directly asserts the ax-contract null case: gate timeout serializes exit_code as null (L22) and gate messages report seconds / cmd name. gateFailureMessage signature updated to take *model.Workflow (the +4/-4 reroute).

Covers §3.6/§10.11 import presence: bare-array force preserves target workflow, wrapped-without-key preserves, wrapped-with-key wins, and a new bare-array import writes an empty {} block with no materialized defaults (asserts nil review_clean_rounds/gate_timeout_seconds, empty block).

runImport presence-preserving (§3.6): bare array -> WorkflowOverride{} (empty -> {}); target-exists + no workflow key carries over existing block (L125-128); wrapped-with-key preserved via ReadTaskFile. Exit codes correct: bare array w/o --spec -> ExitUsage(2); invalid JSON -> ExitFile(3); validation errors -> ExitValidation(1). Output {imported,path}.

runInit writes WorkflowOverride{} -> sparse {} block (§3.3/§5.1); quality_gate/commit_strategy set only when the flag is non-empty. runEjectRoles exit codes: unknown domain -> ExitUsage(2), existing file -> ExitFile(3)+hint. written:=make([]string,0) so the 'ejected' array serializes [] not null.
Covers §10.1: no flags -> {}, --quality-gate -> {quality_gate}, --commit-strategy -> {commit_strategy}, both compose; asserts the exact workflow map with no materialized default fields.

L160 resolves the plan workflow via engine.ResolveWorkflowLayers WITHOUT clamping tf.Workflow first, unlike done.go/config.go which go through EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile / ResolveEffectiveWorkflow (both clamp out-of-range overrides per §3.5/§7.1). For an authored or imported out-of-range value (e.g. review_clean_rounds:0), tp plan reports the raw out-of-range value as if effective — diverging from tp config --resolved and from what tp review enforces — and emits no 'out of range' warning. Narrow edge case: in-range/absent overrides produce identical, correct output.

runSetWorkflow exit codes match §6.1: out-of-range/non-parseable integer and bad checks -> ExitValidation(1) (L307,312,326,332); read-only quality_gate/commit_strategy and unknown field -> ExitUsage(2) (L293,298). Validation runs before any write, so a rejected set leaves the file unmodified. Presence-preserving: sets only the named pointer, siblings untouched (L355-374); explicit empty checks normalized to []Check{} -> present []. Output {updated:{...}} deterministic (encoding/json sorts map keys); runSetBulk results:=make(...,0) so results is [] not null.
